items.back: Spacing between estimated positions must never be less than the prescribed minimum. If one aircraft requires a different time-based standard, apply the larger of the two.

items.front: 8-3-1: Controller action when estimate update shows minimum lost
items.back: Ensure separation is reestablished and issue traffic information as necessary.
Applied as prescribed in Chapter 6, Nonradar.

items.front: 8-3-2: Three track types for longitudinal separation
items.back: 1. Same track/course. 2. Crossing tracks/courses. 3. Reciprocal tracks/courses.

Vertically separate for the applicable minimum time interval before AND after estimated passing. Vertical may discontinue only when both aircraft report passed a significant point with same-direction spacing, OR both report passed ground-based NAVAIDs/DME fixes indicating they have passed each other.

items.front: 8-3-2: Same track vs crossing track measurement point
items.back: Same track: spacing between aircraft continuously along track. Crossing track: spacing at the point of intersection.

items.front: 8-3-3: Four conditions for Mach number technique
items.back: 1. Turbojet aircraft only. 2. Same track or continuously diverging tracks. 3. Aircraft in level, climbing, or descending flight. 4. Mach number (or range) issued to each aircraft unless ICAO regional agreement prescribes otherwise.

items.front: 8-3-3: Mach technique longitudinal minima when preceding equals or exceeds following Mach
8-3-3: Reduced minima when preceding aircraft is faster
items.back: 9 min (Mach 0.02 faster), 8 min (0.03), 7 min (0.04), 6 min (0.05), 5 min (0.06). All require preceding aircraft maintaining a Mach number greater than the following.

items.front: 8-3-3: Pilot obligations under Mach number technique
items.back: Strictly adhere to last assigned Mach number even during climbs/descents unless revised by ATC. Request ATC approval before changes. If immediate change essential (turbulence), notify ATC as soon as possible.

items.front: 8-3-3: Rule-of-thumb for Mach technique when following aircraft is faster
items.back: For each 600 NM distance, add 1 minute for each 0.01 Mach difference. See TBL 8-3-1 for full matrix.
